title: Advanced React Patterns and State Management
summary: Sharpen React skills with hooks, component patterns, and state management critical for building scalable frontend apps.
targetSkill: Experience with React and modern frontend frameworks
personalizationNote: Deepen React expertise to complement backend proficiency and build richer user interfaces.
videoStyle: Code examples with rendered component previews
learningObjectives: Manage state with context and reducers
knowledgeChecks: What hook manages side-effects in React?
How does Context API help with state?
narrationScript:
Scene 1: React Hooks Refresher
useState creates local state; useEffect runs side-effects like data fetching. Custom hooks encapsulate reusable logic. Remember, hooks must be called at the top level.

Scene 2: State Management Patterns
Context shares state globally without passing props deeply. useReducer manages complex state updates predictably, ideal for forms or apps with many interactions.

Scene 3: Performance Optimizations
React.memo skips rendering unchanged components. useMemo caches expensive calculations. Lazy loading splits your code, loading components only when needed.

slides.title: React Hooks Refresher
slides.speakerNotes: Hooks are the heart of modern React. We'll review their correct usage to manage state and lifecycle efficiently.
slides.narration: useState creates local state; useEffect runs side-effects like data fetching. Custom hooks encapsulate reusable logic. Remember, hooks must be called at the top level.
slides.visualCue: Hook usage code snippet alongside live output.

slides.title: State Management Patterns
slides.speakerNotes: Managing state across components is essential. Learn to use context and reducers to avoid prop-drilling and keep code manageable.
slides.narration: Context shares state globally without passing props deeply. useReducer manages complex state updates predictably, ideal for forms or apps with many interactions.
slides.visualCue: State flow diagram and code snippets demonstrating context provider.

slides.title: Performance Optimizations
slides.speakerNotes: Boost app responsiveness by preventing wasted renders and loading resources on demand.
slides.narration: React.memo skips rendering unchanged components. useMemo caches expensive calculations. Lazy loading splits your code, loading components only when needed.
slides.visualCue: Chart showing component re-render triggers before and after memoization.
